positives: Outstanding noise isolation, blocking out everything from the low airplane rumble to high-pitched A/C whine.
Battery lasts over 31 hours continuously with ANC on.
Can be used both wirelessly and via cable with virtually no latency when wired.
Out of the box, warm sound emphasizing thump and punch, with options to customize via graphic EQ and presets.
Supports multi-device pairing allowing simultaneous connection to computer and smartphone.
negatives: Shallow ear cups can fail to form a proper seal, especially for glasses wearers, reducing noise cancelling effectiveness.

records.recommendation_type: Best Upper Mid-Range Noise Cancelling Headphones
positives: Blocks out more bass-range noise like bus and plane engines than our top pick.
Continuous battery life of over 37 hours with ANC on.
Bass-heavy default sound profile with customizable graphic EQ presets via companion app.
negatives: Microphone performance is thin and muffled, making voice calls suffer.

positives: Adaptive ANC system adjusts to your environment, blocking noise across the spectrum effectively.
Continuous battery life of over 27 hours with ANC on.
V-shaped sound signature with emphasis on bass and treble, customizable via app.
Supports multi-device pairing for simultaneous connections.
negatives: Noticeably high noise floor with ANC on, though mitigated when content is playing.

positives: Noise isolation performance nearly matches the more expensive Space Q45, blocking engine rumble and ambient chatter.
49 hours of continuous battery life with ANC on, surpassing some pricier models.
Comfortable over-ear design with multi-device pairing support.
negatives: Lacks adjustable ANC control and LDAC support found on higher-end Anker models.

positives: Audiophile-approved balanced sound suitable for most audio content.
54 hours of continuous playback time.
Customizable fully parametric five-band EQ and crossfeed via companion app.
USB-C Bluetooth dongle included for high-res audio streaming.
negatives: Poor frequency response consistency compared to top pick.

positives: Comfortable fit with about 44 hours of continuous battery life.
Using 'Transport' mode, outstanding noise isolation of rumbling engines and background chit-chat.
V-shaped sound profile emphasizing thump and boom, adjustable via companion app.
negatives: ANC affects sound signature; audio can sound boomy and hollow when ANC is off.
Leaks quite a bit of sound, which may disturb nearby listeners.

records.recommendation_type: Best Over-Ear Headphones Under $100 For Calls
positives: Integrated mic makes voice clear and natural, separating it from ambient sound.
Long continuous battery life of more than 40 hours.
Balanced sound profile with detailed mid-range ensuring clear voices.
negatives: Comfortable design can feel tight for larger heads.
ANC performance is weak compared to competitors.

positives: Comparable ANC performance to the Q30 Wireless at a lower price.
Blocks rumbling bus engines, plane cabin din, chatty passengers, and squeaky brakes with combined ANC and passive isolation.
Battery lasts almost 32 hours with ANC on.
Features Quick Attention transparency mode and location-based listening settings.
Lightweight with squishy earpads for long-term comfort.
negatives: Not ideal for large ears due to smaller ear cup size.

positives: Fantastic ANC that reduces bass-range noises like bus and plane engines.
37 hours of continuous battery life with ANC on.
Lightweight, well-padded fit suitable for long trips.
negatives: Disappointing microphone performance for on-the-go calls.

positives: Adaptive ANC blocks out rumbling engines nearly as well as the Sony XM6.
V-shaped sound profile with extra emphasis on bass and treble.
27 hours of continuous battery life with ANC on and optional wired mode.
negatives: Shorter battery life and less comfortable build than higher-end competitors.

positives: ANC performance similar to the Space Q45 at a significantly lower price.
About 44 hours of continuous battery life.
Well-built and comfortable for long commutes.
negatives: Leaks much more audio than higher-end models, making music audible to bystanders.

positives: Solid ANC performance that reduces engine noise and background chatter.
Affordable price with reliable noise cancelling features.
negatives: ANC doesn't block as much bass-range noise as more expensive models.

positives: The WH-1000XM6 delivers very strong ANC performance, ranking among the top models in our ANC isolation charts.
It offers extremely good passive isolation across the frequency range.
The companion app allows users to tailor sound preferences with robust customization options.
negatives: Its connection options feel dated compared to newer models.
A pronounced spike at 10 kHz in the frequency response is difficult to EQ away.

positives: The headphones reduced bus engine, train screech, and airplane noise significantly, blocking a great amount of ambient noise across the entire frequency range in testing.
The large dense ear pads create an excellent seal on the head, providing excellent passive noise isolation.
They feature premium audio quality at a competitive price, along with wireless connectivity support for Snapdragon Sound and aptX Lossless.
negatives: The companion app is simplistic, lacking advanced customization features.

positives: They offer better ANC capabilities than the Sennheiser Momentum 4 Wireless in our tests.
They include the BTD 700 USB-C adapter, enabling higher-bitrate Bluetooth performance on any USB-C port.
negatives: Priced $50 higher than competing Sony and Bose models, making them a less cost-effective option.
Their ANC performance is comparatively weaker than other top-tier headphones.
They use the older Bluetooth 5.2 standard, which may become dated for power users requiring LE Audio.

positives: The hybrid active noise cancelation system provides optimal noise attenuation among the best pure noise cancellers tested.
They support Adaptive EQ, adjusting frequency response in real time based on fit to maintain consistent sound quality.
negatives: Their high cost makes them a less accessible option compared to similarly performing competitors.
They support only SBC and AAC codecs, lacking higher-bitrate options like LDAC.

positives: Provides good passive noise isolation thanks to well-fitting ear pads that reduce ambient noise.
Active noise canceling effectively nullifies low-frequency engine rumbles when commuting.
The default audio tuning is well-balanced and scored impressively well in perceptual tests.
Offers a comfortable fit with plush padding, battery life exceeding 31 hours, LDAC support, and wired USB audio connectivity.
negatives: The non-folding design reduces portability for travelers and commuters.
They lack a customizable EQ, limiting fine-tuning of the audio.

positives: It includes a SMART Tx accessory that can transmit audio over Auracast to multiple devices or provide analog input via USB.
They maintain noise cancelation performance within one to two percent of the Apple AirPods Max.
negatives: Sound quality is lacking compared to other top-range models, requiring EQ adjustments or the SMART Tx accessory to improve clarity.
The feature set and price point may not justify a pure value buy compared to sale prices on competing models.

positives: They sound decent and offer solid ANC performance.
negatives: They are a bit dated compared to newer models.

positives: They build on the PX7 with a comprehensive suite of premium features.
negatives: They carry an enormous price tag, making them less accessible.

positives: They achieve 10 to 20 dB of ANC attenuation below 700 Hz, reducing 50 to 75% of low-frequency noise.
The ear cups create an outstanding seal, physically blocking up to 46 dB of high-frequency noise.
They offer robust connectivity options and include a high-quality travel case.
They provide good noise canceling performance suitable for travel.
They are affordable, sleek, and designed to fit smaller heads.
They limit maximum volume to help protect children's hearing.
They offer sound quality that outperforms many top-tier ANC models.
They deliver battery life that exceeds most competitors.
negatives: Their ANC performance is technically behind other top-tier headphones.

positives: They offer a balanced ANC headphone experience at a mid-range price point.
They balance app support, good battery life, and solid functionality as a mid-tier option.
These scored joint-top in my noise-cancellation tests, matching the Bose Ultra 2nd Gen and marginally beating the Sony WH-1000XM6, but can be found for around $100 less than those models.
They're very light, you can wear them for long periods without any fatigue, and they fold down small for travel.
These give you really punchy and dynamic audio, with big (but not overly heavy) bass, zinging treble, and great clarity through the mid-range.
negatives: The 24-hour battery life is below average. If you want 30 hours, the Sony WH-1000XM6 or Bose QuietComfort Ultra 2nd Gen will give this to you with the same level of noise cancellation.
The Sony WH-1000XM6 offer a little more detail, a balance of bass/mid/treble that's closer to 'neutral', and a little better cohesion from bass through to mids.

positives: In noise-cancellation tests, I scored these only a couple of points lower than far more expensive models, including Bose's own Ultra models, the Sony WH-1000XM6, and the Sonos Ace, and rated them higher than the AirPods Max when weighting real-world tests higher.
They sound good with rich bass, strong detail, and clear highs, and you can improve sound balance by boosting the mids a couple of points in the app's EQ.
These headphones are really light and comfortable for long-term use, matching the Sony WH-1000XM4's comfort despite being foldable for portability.
negatives: The battery life is just 24 hours with ANC on, which is below average compared to competitors offering up to 60 hours.
The mids are thin and require boosting via EQ settings to achieve a balanced soundstage.
The design is utilitarian and plain, lacking aesthetic appeal with more seams and square edges compared to premium options.

positives: I scored these at basically the same level of ANC power as the Sony WH-1000XM4 or Cambridge Audio P100 SE, even though they cost less than half the price.
They deliver a full-chested mid-range that effectively overcomes background noise, avoiding the need to crank the volume for speech content.
They provide up to 65 hours of continuous use with ANC turned on, the best battery life among all headphones tested.
negatives: The companion app is confusing and disorganized, making customization less intuitive.
The button controls feel cheap and lack premium tactile feedback.
The overall build quality feels slightly flimsy compared to higher-end models.

positives: Battery life is stellar, coming in at 95 hours with ANC off and around 58 hours with active noise cancellation enabled.
The Soundpeats Cove Pro are incredibly comfortable to wear, aided by a surprisingly solid build quality for the price.
For a $50 budget, the ANC provided strips back background noise enough for office or commute use.
negatives: The audio quality is relatively muddy and lacks the clarity of pricier options.
The ANC isn't exceptional and won't entirely block aggressive background sounds, only stripping them back.
There is no 3.5mm port, limiting wired connectivity options.
